labels.1232: Positive descriptive adjectives or phrases praising qualities of brands, products, or people.
labels.4001: Table or list formatting with pipe symbols and categorical property values.
labels.4239: Phrases expressing functional benefits or qualities that solve a problem or improve an experience.
labels.4966: Contrast between two clauses or actions connected by conjunctions like "but" or "and" that present opposing or alternative ideas.
labels.6333: Tokens appearing between delimiters that represent parts of usernames, product codes, or identifiers being split across formatting boundaries.
labels.6678: Repeated word or phrase appears just before and after the firing region, creating a pattern of duplication or contrast.
labels.7093: Names or titles of people, often with first and last names separated by middle initials or additional descriptors.
labels.7309: Asterisks or special characters used as visual separators between distinct text sections.
labels.7416: Transitions between categorical data fields or metadata sections separated by pipe delimiters and asterisks.
labels.7720: Names of pairs or individuals appearing in familial/biographical relationship lists.
labels.9491: Numeric sequences or ID codes appearing between structural delimiters in product/form data.
labels.9788: Phrases comparing or contrasting two things with "also been" or similar comparative constructions about change or alternatives.
labels.11071: Names or initials of people appearing in lists or credits separated by commas or conjunctions.
labels.11310: Negation or qualification phrases that soften or contradict the preceding statement.
labels.12072: The feature activates on phrases where a consequence or result follows an action or condition.
labels.12076: Phrases indicating change, addition, or modification of scope between two concepts or actions.
labels.13133: Transitions between product/promotional content sections and new content categories or disclaimers.
labels.14060: Tokens following a negation or contrast ("but", "not") that introduce the main point or consequence of the preceding clause.
labels.15166: Transitions between clauses or phrases where the feature marks boundaries between different grammatical or semantic units.
labels.15232: The feature activates on contiguous digit sequences that appear within or adjacent to product codes, SKUs, or identification numbers.
labels.16516: Contrast or comparison between two related concepts separated by "and" or similar connectives.
labels.17616: Titles or names of creative works (albums, books, films) appear between the marked tokens.
labels.17765: Contrasting two alternatives presented as opposing choices or options.
labels.17813: Phrases indicating a process or action has already been completed or negated ("had not yet", "is set", "is carefully").
labels.18010: Names or titles of people (first and last names, professional titles, or organizational affiliations).
